# How We Test Cold Email Signals with Clay
**Channel:** Eric Nowoslawski
**URL:** https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3THIdISjTkk
**Date:** 2026-03-26
**Content source:** auto-generated transcript

## Summary
Eric Nowoslawski challenges the over-reliance on intent signals in cold email outreach and presents a data-driven testing methodology using Clay. Rather than guessing which signals matter upfront, he advocates for running three tiered campaigns and enriching positive responses with signal data to identify what actually drives conversions.

## Key Points & Strategies

- **Three-Level Campaign Testing Structure:**
  1. Broad campaign targeting anyone in your TAM with valid ICP match
  2. Segmented campaign narrowed by industry, headcount, job title, department
  3. Signal-specific campaign targeting high-confidence triggers (new roles, product launches)

- **Reverse Signal Validation:** Instead of pre-filtering leads by signals, let people respond naturally to campaigns, then enrich positive responses with signal data to identify patterns

- **Signal Enrichment on Response:** Run comprehensive signal verification (news, tech stack changes, team composition ratios, hiring activity) only on leads who actually responded—reducing wasted effort and cost

- **Comparative Analysis:** Compare signal data between positive and negative responses to understand what truly correlates with conversion vs. what's just correlation

- **Volume-Agnostic Approach:** Methodology works whether sending 2,000 or 10,000 emails daily

- **Challenge the Hype:** Better offer/easier-to-say-yes-to value prop often eliminates need for complex signal targeting

## Workflows & Tools Discussed

- **Clay:** Primary platform for signal enrichment and cross-referencing; accepts positive response data into dedicated tables
- **Clay integrations/features mentioned:**
  - Sculptor (data extraction)
  - Cloud Code
  - CodeEx (insight generation)
  - Signal checklist automation
- **External data sources:** Builtwith (tech stack detection), news/LinkedIn monitoring, hiring signals
- **Clay Agent:** Referenced for automated signal lookup and enrichment

## Use Cases Covered

- Testing whether "new in role" signals drive higher response rates than generic outreach
- Comparing hiring announcements vs. product launches as conversion triggers
- Identifying team composition ratios (AEs to SDRs) that correlate with positive responses
- Detecting LinkedIn activity timing (posting about new news within campaign window)
- Validating custom signal hypotheses without pre-campaign filtering

## Quotes Worth Noting

> "The problem with this is you don't know if a signal is actually worth pursuing or if the person that you reached out to just was working at a company that kind of just needed your thing and it was just another person that would have said yes if you had just emailed them anyway."

> "Instead of running a campaign towards one signal or to our entire TAM or segment, let's just test when they positively respond, what is true about all of the companies... Let them respond to the campaign for any reason, right? It doesn't matter why they responded, but then when you get the positive responses, enrich them for every idea you might have."
